entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Using a survey of key events in the American church, articles, and extra resources on Christianity across the world in the lesson plans, this course gives students a personal and big picture look at church history during the 19th century. A second text is used to bring the people and events of the early church to life.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Bible: Grades 10-11, Bible: Grade 12

rows.label: Church History: Grades 10-12
rows.books: When the Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers||Turning Points in American Church History

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Bible: Grade 12
☐ Make or purchase Bible journals for Old and New Testament studies (see some suggestions in the following course Extra Helpings: Bible: Grades 7-8, 9, 10-11, and 12).

entries.title: Term Prep & Teacher Tips
entries.content:
Church History: Grades 10-12
Check the Teacher Notes for content guidance.

books.title: When the Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers
Turning Points in American Church History

blocks.text: Take a moment to recall what you read previously in the same book or topic.

Read the passage assigned or read for the number of minutes allocated. If a book is challenging for you, try stopping to narrate to yourself silently now and then to make sure you are tracking. Or you could listen to an audiobook version of the text and read along. You may also want to read aloud to yourself. If a book is an easy read, enjoy the ride! Keep an eye out for quotes to put in your Commonplace Book or events or characters to put down in your Book of Centuries.

Process and integrate your reading by narrating either orally or in writing. Some lesson plans will give you a suggestion of how best to narrate. You might want to set a timer to keep yourself on track, so you stop reading with enough time to narrate.

Read notes in your Study Bible or commentary. Look up places mentioned in the text in your Bible atlas. Add something to your Book of Centuries or Commonplace Book. Jot down additional reflections and thoughts in your Bible notebook or share with others.

Read over the lesson plans to familiarize yourself with the content for accountability and interest. If doing the lessons together, use a picture, a map, or an engaging question to rouse students' interest before reading. You may also highlight one or two unfamiliar terms if needed. If in a classroom context, you may choose to leave larger doctrinal conversations for students to have with their parents at home.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Turning Points in American Church History: How Pivotal Events Shaped a Nation and a Faith, Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Chapter 7 The Benevolent Empire, p.131-151

➜Identify three ideas that were new to you from this chapter and explain why those ideas captured your attention.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Chapter 1 The Church Fathers: Who Is Included?, p.1-4
and
Chapter 2 Clement and the Corinthian Coup, p. 5-10

➜ NARRATE
Explain how developing a literary relationship with the early Church Fathers can impact a person's spiritual life.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch. 3 The Didache: Out of the Mist of History, p.11-17

➜ WRITE
If you were writing a catechism, what three areas of instruction would you prioritize first and why?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch. 4 Ignatius: Prophet, Pastor, and Witness, p.18-28

➜ CHARACTER SKETCH
Develop a character sketch of Ignatius and share it with a family member or friend.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.5 The Martyrdom of Polycarp, p.29-37

➜ DEFINE & REFLECT
Define martyrdom and reflect on the impact martyrdom has on the church.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.6 Diogenetus and the Mystery of Christian Newness, p.38-46

➜ WRITE
Write down the questions the "mystery author" answered, and respond to those questions in your own words.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.7 Justin and the Philospoher's Cloak, p.47-57

➜ EXPLAIN
Explain why Justin considered himself a philosopher. Why did Justin refer to Christ as a "key that unlocked all doors..."

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 8
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.8 Irenaeus and the Gnostic Threat, p.58-67

➜ LIST
In your journal, identify the heresies that Irenaeus confronted, and make a list of the potential heresies the church is battling against today. Are there similarities between today's heresies and those that Irenaeus confronted?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.9 Irenaeus and the Battle for the Bible, p.68-76

➜EXPLAIN
Explain how the canon of Scripture was established to a friend or family member.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.10 Clement and the School of Alexandria, p.77-87

➜ CONTRAST
Contrast gnosticism with Clement's understand of a "true" gnostic.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 11
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.11 Origen: Zeal and Genius, p.88-101

➜ DESCRIBE
In your journal, describe the catechetical institute of Alexandria and include the academic disciplines that were studied. Why did the institute include disciplines beyond biblical studies?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 12
lessons.body:
Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course. Question will come from: When The Church Was Young

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Turning Points in American Church History: How Pivotal Events Shaped a Nation and a Faith

➜ READ
Ch. 8 Houses Divided, p.153-172

➜ IDENTIFY
Identify three ideas that captured your attention and explain why.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.12 The Tragedy of Tertullian, p.102-112

➜ DESCRIBE
Describe Tertullian's views on Christian persecution and martyrdom.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.13 Cyprian and the Unity of the Church, p.113-124

➜ NARRATE
In your journal, identify what concerned Cyprian the most.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.14 Hippolytus and the Lex Orandi, p.125-138

➜ CONTRAST
In your notebook, contrast transformation of behavior with intellectual understanding of doctrine.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 17
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.15 The Great Persecution, p.139-149

➜ WRITE
In your journal, write about something that caught your attention.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.16 Nicea, the First Ecumenical Council, p.150-161

➜ EXPLAIN
Explain to a family member or a friend why the Council of Nicaea was convened.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.17 Athanasius against the World, p.162-178

➜ DESCRIBE
How would you describe Athanasius's character to someone who has never heard of him?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.18 Basil the Great, p.179-191

➜ WRITE
Write a "Rule of Life" that would challenge you to become more disciplined and intentional with your time and share it with a family member or friend.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.19 Two Gregorys and a Council, p.192-199 "the imperial capital"

➜ DESCRIBE
In your journal, describe the type of relationship Gregory and Basil cultivated.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 22
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.19 Two Gregorys and a Council, p.199 "Basil had helped" - p.209

➜ COMPARE & CONTRAST
Compare and contrast the two Gregorys.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 23
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.20 Ambrose of Milan: The Jonah of the West, p.210-222

➜ DISCUSS
Identify something that stood out to you from the reading.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 24
lessons.body:
Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course. Questions will come from: When The Church Was Young

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Turning Points in American Church History: How Pivotal Events Shaped a Nation and a Faith, Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.9, p.173-192

➜ WRITE
Select one of the personal letters (p.178 or p.184) and write a response letter to the individual.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.21 Augustine and Amazing Grace, p.223-232 "to contend with"

➜ DESCRIBE
Describe the impact Augustine's mother had on his life.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 27
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.21 Augustine and Amazing Grace, p.233 "Ten years after" - p.242

➜ WRITE
Write about something from the reading that caught your attention.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 28
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.22 The Preacher with the Golden Tongue, p.243-255

➜ LIST
Make a list of the reforms John implemented to refocus the church on its mission. Are any of those reforms need today?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 29
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.23 Jerome and the Bible, p.256-265

➜ WRITE
In your journal, write about something from the reading that caught your attention.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 30
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.24 Leo and Peter, p.266-274

➜ DESCRIBE 
Describe how Leo approached his role as pope, and explain why he was given such a unique title.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 31
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.25 Gregory the Great, p.275-279 "centuries to come"

➜ WRITE
In your journal, write about something from the reading that grabbed your attention.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 32
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.25 Gregory the Great, p.279 "Gregory ranked effective" - p.285

➜ LIST
List the qualities or skills Gregory the Great expected every pastor to develop.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 33
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.26 The Voice in the Voices, p.286-289 "Gospel of Thomas, were not."

➜ DISCUSS
Read the following passage and explain what the author is communicating:
"What they passed on was not primarily a book, or a set of truths. It was a life. It was the truth...There are some things that you can only learn by absorbing them. You absorb such a thing by living with people who have it. And once absorbed, you can never fully articulate it. But you can live it, and you can pass it on to those you live with."

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 34
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.26 The Voice of the Voices, p.289 "The most fundamental" - 294

➜ WRITE
In your journal, write about something in the reading that caused you to pause and reflect.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 35
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Postscript When the Church Was Young, p.295-296

➜ CATCH UP
You use this week to catch up on previous readings or assignments.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 10-12 - Lesson 36
lessons.body:
Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course. Questions will come from: When The Church Was Young
